title: strychnos andamanensis
intro: Strychnos andamanensis, also known as the Andaman strychnine tree or poison tree, is a plant species native to the Andaman and Nicobar Islands in India. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include being a natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic agent. The health benefits of Strychnos andamanensis include treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as being used as a diuretic to treat urinary problems. The plant is often prepared in herbal forms such as decoctions, infusions, and powders, which are used in traditional medicine to treat a range of conditions.
